meta_title: Impact Doors in Tequesta, FL | Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
meta_description: Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs hurricane-rated impact doors in Tequesta, FL. ESW & CWI brands, 170mph wind-rated, licensed & insured. Free quotes – financing from $87/mo.
h1: Impact Doors in Tequesta, FL — Coastal Protection You Can Count On
intro:
Tequesta is one of Palm Beach County's most desirable coastal communities, and with that distinction comes a very real responsibility to protect your home from the forces of nature that define South Florida living. Tucked just north of Jupiter Inlet and sitting roughly two miles from the Atlantic coastline, Tequesta homeowners in neighborhoods like Tequesta Pines, Lighthouse Cove, and River Walk face annual hurricane season with a level of exposure that demands more than standard building materials. When the next major storm approaches — and in South Florida, it's always a matter of when, not if — the last thing you want standing between your family and 170-mph wind gusts is a conventional hollow-core door or an aging sliding glass panel that was never designed with today's storm intensity in mind.

With an average home value of approximately $510,000, Tequesta properties represent a significant financial and emotional investment. Impact doors are no longer a luxury upgrade reserved for new construction — they are a smart, code-compliant protection strategy that delivers immediate and long-term returns. Whether you're safeguarding a waterfront retreat near Coral Cove Park or a family home tucked into the quieter streets off Seabrook Road, every entry point on your home is a potential vulnerability during hurricane season. A properly installed impact-resistant entry door, sliding glass door, or French door system dramatically reduces the risk of catastrophic pressure loss, water intrusion, and structural failure during a direct hit or near-miss storm event.

At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing — South Florida's Kings of Service — we've spent decades helping homeowners across Palm Beach, Broward, and Miami-Dade counties make the transition to impact-rated protection. Our team understands Tequesta's unique building environment: the Village's permitting requirements, the wind zone designations, the aging housing stock (nearly 45% of homes were built before 1994), and the insurance landscape that rewards proactive upgrades. If your home still has original doors from the pre-Andrew era, the time to act is now — before the next storm puts your property and peace of mind to the test.

section_why.h2: Why Tequesta Homeowners Trust Premier Impact Windows & Roofing for Their Impact Door Installations
section_why.body:
Choosing the right contractor for impact door installation in Tequesta isn't just about finding someone who can swing a hammer. It's about partnering with a team that understands the local building environment, respects the permitting process through the Village of Tequesta Building Department, and has the hands-on experience to deliver a flawless installation that will perform under real storm conditions. At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, we bring over 60 years of combined experience to every project, and every single installation is performed by our own in-house crew — never subcontracted, never rushed, always done right.

Our 100% in-house installation model is one of the things that truly sets us apart in a crowded South Florida market. When you hire Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, the same experienced team that shows up on day one is the team that sees your project through to final inspection. We don't hand off your home to a third-party labor crew unfamiliar with our products or your expectations. This matters enormously for impact door installations, where precision in framing, anchoring, sealing, and hardware alignment directly determines whether a door performs at its rated wind load during a storm. Our technicians are factory-trained on both the ESW (Eastern Storm Windows) and CWI product lines we install throughout Palm Beach County.

With over 500 completed homes across the region and an A+ rating from the Better Business Bureau, our track record speaks for itself. Homeowners in Tequesta Pines, River Walk, and Lighthouse Cove have come to rely on our team not only for the quality of our work but for the transparency of our process — from the initial free consultation and accurate quote to permit pulling, installation, inspection, and post-project follow-up. We understand that impact door replacement in a home valued around $510,000 is not a casual purchase, and we treat every project with the care and attention it deserves.

We're also proud to be fully licensed and insured, which protects you throughout the entire installation process. In a marketplace where fly-by-night storm contractors are common — especially in the aftermath of hurricanes — working with a licensed, BBB-accredited company with deep local roots in Palm Beach County gives Tequesta homeowners the confidence that comes from accountability. We're not just passing through; we're your neighbors, and our reputation is on the line with every door we install.

section_history.h2: Tequesta's Storm History Makes the Case for Impact Door Upgrades
section_history.body:
Tequesta's geographic position near the northern edge of Palm Beach County places it squarely in the historical path of some of the most destructive hurricanes to strike South Florida over the past three decades. Since 1990, the Tequesta area has experienced eight significant storm events, including three that left lasting impressions on the community and its housing stock. Hurricane Frances arrived in September 2004, bringing sustained winds and prolonged rainfall that exposed vulnerabilities in doors, windows, and roofing systems throughout the village. Many homes with standard aluminum sliding glass doors suffered water infiltration and structural damage as storm surge and wind-driven rain found the weakest entry points. Just over a year later, Hurricane Wilma in October 2005 delivered one of the most intense pressure gradient events ever recorded in Palm Beach County, with rapid wind acceleration that overwhelmed conventional door hardware and frame systems across the region.

Then came Hurricane Irma in September 2017 — a broad, powerful storm that served as a stark reminder for Tequesta residents that complacency between storm seasons can be costly. While Irma's direct impacts in northern Palm Beach County were less catastrophic than those in Miami-Dade, the combination of sustained tropical-storm-force winds, storm surge near the Jupiter Inlet, and falling debris caused widespread damage to properties that had not been upgraded to current hurricane protection standards. Homeowners who had installed impact doors prior to Irma reported minimal damage and no structural breaches, while neighbors with original 1980s and early 1990s entry systems faced broken frames, blown-out glass panels, and significant water damage.

These three storms — Frances, Wilma, and Irma — represent more than historical data points. They are real-world proof that Tequesta's coastal proximity and wind zone exposure demand serious, code-compliant hurricane protection at every door opening in your home. With nearly half of all homes in the area predating the post-Andrew building code reforms of 1994, the window of opportunity to upgrade before the next major storm is not unlimited.

section_code.h2: Florida Building Code Requirements for Impact Doors in Tequesta and Palm Beach County
section_code.body:
While Tequesta falls outside the High-Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ) — a designation primarily applied to Miami-Dade and Broward counties — the village is still subject to rigorous Florida Building Code requirements that mandate hurricane-rated products for all new and replacement door installations. Tequesta sits within a designated 170-mph wind zone, which means any impact door installed must be tested and approved to withstand design wind pressures consistent with that rating. These are not optional upgrades; they are legally required standards that protect both the occupants and the structural integrity of the home. Doors that carry Miami-Dade Notice of Acceptance (NOA) approval or Florida Product Approval listings are the benchmark for compliance in this wind corridor.

The permitting process in Tequesta runs through the Village of Tequesta Building Department, and all impact door replacements require a building permit prior to installation. This means product approval documentation, installation drawings, and contractor licensing must be submitted and reviewed before work begins. Attempting to install impact doors without a permit is not only a code violation — it can void manufacturer warranties, create problems during home sales, and result in costly removal and reinstallation. At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, we handle the entire permit process on your behalf, from initial application through to final inspection sign-off, so you never have to navigate the bureaucracy alone.

For homes built before 1994 — which accounts for roughly 45% of Tequesta's residential housing stock — there are additional considerations around structural opening reinforcement. Older door frames may require framing upgrades or header reinforcement to properly anchor a new impact door system and achieve the required wind load ratings. Our in-house installation team conducts a thorough pre-installation assessment to identify any such requirements upfront, ensuring your project is fully compliant with both the Florida Building Code and the Village of Tequesta's local amendments.

section_products.h2: ESW and CWI Impact Doors: Premium Hurricane Protection Built for Tequesta's Coastal Conditions
section_products.body:
Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs two of the industry's most respected impact door product lines — ESW (Eastern Storm Windows) and CWI — both of which are engineered specifically for South Florida's demanding coastal environment. These are not off-the-shelf products adapted for hurricane use; they are purpose-built systems designed to meet and exceed the wind pressure ratings required in Palm Beach County's 170-mph wind zone. From impact-resistant entry doors to sliding glass doors and elegant French door configurations, both ESW and CWI offer a comprehensive range of styles and configurations that allow Tequesta homeowners to enhance their curb appeal without compromising on structural performance.

ESW impact doors feature heavy-duty aluminum frames with thermally broken profiles, laminated safety glass with PVB interlayers that hold the pane intact even under impact, and multi-point locking systems that engage multiple points along the door frame simultaneously. This multi-point lock design is critical — it distributes wind load forces across the entire frame rather than concentrating stress at a single latch point, dramatically improving resistance to blow-in during sustained wind events. ESW's product line has earned Florida Product Approval listings across a wide range of door sizes and configurations, making them suitable for everything from a single entry door replacement in Tequesta Pines to a full set of large-format sliding glass doors overlooking the Loxahatchee River in River Walk.

CWI impact doors bring their own set of strengths to the Palm Beach County market, including options with enhanced energy efficiency ratings, decorative glass configurations for French door applications, and reinforced sill systems that significantly reduce water infiltration risk during storm-driven rain events. For Tequesta homeowners near Lighthouse Cove or along the waterfront corridors near Coral Cove Park, CWI's combination of storm performance and architectural versatility offers an ideal solution. Both product lines come with manufacturer warranties and are backed by Premier Impact Windows & Roofing's installation guarantee, giving you complete confidence in both the product and the craftsmanship behind it.

section_cost.h2: Impact Door Costs and Financing Options for Tequesta Homeowners
section_cost.body:
One of the first questions homeowners ask when considering impact door installation is: what's the investment going to look like? For a Tequesta home valued around $510,000, the scope of the project varies based on the number of door openings, door type (entry, sliding glass, or French), glass configuration, and any structural preparation work required for pre-1994 homes. As a general benchmark, a single impact-rated entry door replacement typically ranges from $2,500 to $5,000 installed, while larger sliding glass door systems or multi-panel French door configurations can range from $4,000 to $10,000 or more depending on size and specification. A full-home door replacement project for a mid-size Tequesta residence might total between $8,000 and $20,000 — a figure that sounds significant until you weigh it against the cost of storm damage, insurance savings, and property value appreciation.

To make that investment accessible, Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers flexible financing options starting at just $87 per month, with $0 down payment options available for qualified applicants. Our financing programs are designed to put hurricane protection within reach for homeowners who want to act now rather than wait until budget constraints align perfectly with storm season. Spreading the cost over time while immediately benefiting from the insurance discounts and energy savings that impact doors deliver means many homeowners find the net monthly cost is considerably more manageable than the sticker price suggests.

From a return-on-investment standpoint, impact doors in Palm Beach County typically deliver on multiple fronts simultaneously. Insurance premium reductions (more on that in the next section) can save hundreds of dollars annually. Impact doors also contribute to better thermal efficiency, reducing energy costs year-round in Tequesta's subtropical climate. And when it comes time to sell, homes equipped with full impact protection consistently command stronger offers and faster closings in a market where buyers are increasingly savvy about storm preparedness costs.

section_insurance.h2: Insurance Savings and Wind Mitigation Credits for Impact Door Upgrades in Tequesta
section_insurance.body:
One of the most financially compelling reasons to install impact doors in Tequesta is the direct impact on your homeowner's insurance premiums. Florida's insurance market has been under significant stress in recent years, with carriers withdrawing from the market, non-renewing policies, and raising rates across Palm Beach County. Homeowners who can demonstrate upgraded wind mitigation features — including impact-rated doors — are in a much stronger position to retain coverage with preferred carriers and secure meaningful premium discounts. Under Florida law, insurers are required to offer discounts for wind mitigation features verified through an official Wind Mitigation Inspection, and impact-rated doors contribute directly to your home's overall wind resistance score.

For Tequesta homeowners, the wind mitigation credit for impact doors can be substantial. When combined with impact windows and a qualifying roof covering, some homeowners in Palm Beach County see annual premium reductions of 20% to 45% — savings that can amount to several hundred to over a thousand dollars per year depending on your coverage levels and current carrier. Major carriers active in the Palm Beach County market, including Citizens Property Insurance, Universal Property & Casualty, and Heritage Property & Casualty, all recognize wind mitigation credits for properly permitted and inspected impact door installations. Having your permit closed and a passing inspection on file is essential to claiming these credits, which is yet another reason why Premier Impact Windows & Roofing's full-service permit management is so valuable.

Beyond the annual premium savings, impact doors also reduce your exposure to out-of-pocket storm damage costs. In the event of a named hurricane, conventional doors are frequently the point of entry for wind-driven water damage — one of the most expensive categories of storm-related repairs. By eliminating that vulnerability, you're not only protecting your insurance profile but also reducing the likelihood of filing a claim at all. In today's hardening insurance market, maintaining a clean claims history is increasingly important for Tequesta homeowners who want to keep their coverage intact and their rates from escalating further.

faqs.q: Do I really need impact doors if I live in Tequesta and not Miami-Dade's HVHZ?
faqs.a: Absolutely. While Tequesta is not located in the High-Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ), it sits within a designated 170-mph wind zone under Florida Building Code. This means all door replacements must meet hurricane-rated product approval standards. Additionally, Tequesta's coastal proximity — just two miles from the Atlantic — and its storm history including Hurricanes Frances, Wilma, and Irma make impact-rated doors a smart, code-required choice for protecting your home and family.

faqs.q: How much do impact doors cost in Tequesta, and what financing options are available?
faqs.a: Impact door costs in Tequesta typically range from $2,500 to $5,000 for a single entry door installation, while sliding glass door systems and French door configurations can range from $4,000 to $10,000 or more. Full-home projects vary based on number of openings and structural requirements. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers financing starting at just $87 per month with $0 down options for qualified applicants, making it easy to upgrade your protection before the next hurricane season without a large upfront payment.

faqs.q: What permits are required to replace doors with impact doors in Tequesta?
faqs.a: All impact door replacements in Tequesta require a building permit through the Village of Tequesta Building Department. Permit applications must include Florida Product Approval documentation for the doors being installed, installation drawings, and contractor licensing information. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles the entire permitting process on your behalf — from application submission to scheduling the final inspection — so your project is fully code-compliant and properly documented for insurance and resale purposes.

faqs.q: How long does an impact door installation take in Tequesta?
faqs.a: Most single-door replacements can be completed in one day once materials are on-site and the permit is approved. Larger projects involving multiple doors — such as a full entry door, sliding glass doors, and French door replacement — typically take two to three days for installation. The overall project timeline from contract signing to completed final inspection is generally four to eight weeks, with the majority of that time allocated to permit review and material fabrication. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing keeps you informed at every step of the process.

faqs.q: Will impact doors lower my homeowner's insurance in Palm Beach County?
faqs.a: Yes — in most cases, significantly. Florida law requires insurers to offer wind mitigation discounts for homes with impact-rated doors that have been permitted, installed, and verified through a Wind Mitigation Inspection. Tequesta homeowners with full impact protection — including doors and windows — can often see premium reductions of 20% to 45% annually with carriers like Citizens, Universal, and Heritage. The savings frequently offset a large portion of the installation cost over time, making impact doors one of the highest-ROI upgrades available to Palm Beach County homeowners.

cta_body: Contact Premier Impact Windows & Roofing today for a free, no-obligation consultation and quote — our Kings of Service team is ready to help you safeguard your Tequesta home before the next storm season arrives.
